profile.title: Profile
profile.subtitle: Set your Chess.com and Lichess usernames here so they sync across the app.

errors.chesscomFormat: Chess.com username: 3-25 chars, letters, digits, "-" or "_"
errors.lichessFormat: Lichess username: 2-20 chars, letters, digits, "-" or "_"

home.subtitle: Master the basics — pieces, moves, and core strategies.
home.description: The idea behind this project is to help players analyze positions on their own. The Key Positions section surfaces positions where you spent a long time thinking. You can send your own analysis of these to the platform's coaches and get a reply. The platform also includes learning materials, though those are in Russian only. Thanks for visiting!

theory.title: Theory
theory.description: Basic checkmates: two rooks, queen, rook.

pieceValues.title: Piece Values
pieceValues.description: Understand the relative value of each chess piece and why it matters.

games.title: Chess.com Games
games.description: Browse and replay the last 10 games of any Chess.com player.

thinkTime.title: Key Positions
thinkTime.description: Surface every move where you spent 45+ seconds thinking.

twoRooksMate.title: Two-rook checkmate
twoRooksMate.description: The 'ladder' method — two rooks alternately push the opponent's king to the edge of the board. Also known as the linear mate.

queenMate.title: Queen checkmate
queenMate.description: King and queen versus a lone king. The queen restricts space; the king helps to deliver mate.

rookMate.title: Rook checkmate
rookMate.description: King and rook versus a lone king. King opposition plus a cutting line by the rook.

windmill.title: Windmill
windmill.description: Tactical pattern: a sequence of discovered checks by bishop and rook lets you pick off the opponent's pieces one by one.

steinitz.name: Wilhelm Steinitz
steinitz.description: First official World Chess Champion. Founder of the positional school, introduced the principles of accumulating small advantages.

lasker.name: Emanuel Lasker
lasker.description: World champion for 27 years — a record. Known for his psychological approach and universal style.

capablanca.name: José Raúl Capablanca
capablanca.description: Cuban genius with phenomenal intuition. Hardly lost a game during his prime.

alekhine.name: Alexander Alekhine
alekhine.description: Famous for aggressive style and deep combinations. The only champion to die while holding the title.

euwe.name: Max Euwe
euwe.description: Dutch champion and mathematician. Defeated Alekhine in their 1935 match.

botvinnik.name: Mikhail Botvinnik
botvinnik.description: Patriarch of the Soviet chess school. Mentored a whole generation of champions.

smyslov.name: Vasily Smyslov
smyslov.description: Known for his harmonious style and outstanding endgame technique.

tal.name: Mikhail Tal
tal.description: The 'Magician from Riga'. Legendary tactician famed for sacrifices and attacking play.

petrosian.name: Tigran Petrosian
petrosian.description: Master of defense and prophylaxis. One of the toughest players ever to crack.

spassky.name: Boris Spassky
spassky.description: All-rounder. World champion who lost the title to Fischer in 1972.

fischer.name: Bobby Fischer
fischer.description: Chess legend who broke Soviet dominance. World champion in 1972.

karpov.name: Anatoly Karpov
karpov.description: Master of positional play and control. Dominated the 1970s–80s.

kasparov.name: Garry Kasparov
kasparov.description: One of the strongest players in history. Aggressive style and deep opening preparation.

pieceValues.title: Piece Values
pieceValues.howTo: Pick a combination of pieces whose point values add up to the target number.

games.title: Chess.com Games
games.subtitle: Browse the last 10 games of any Chess.com player.

thinkTime.title: Key Positions
thinkTime.subtitle: Positions where you spent extra time thinking. Click a card to open the full game.
thinkTime.empty: No moves crossed the {threshold}s threshold in your last 10 games.
thinkTime.showing: {count, plural, one {# long think} other {# long thinks}} across {gameCount, plural, one {# game} other {# games}}

error.title: Something went wrong
